name: Puerto El Caballón descends Villanueva de Córdoba — circular by Pozoblanco
name_original: Puerto El Caballón desce Villanueva de Córdoba — circular por Pozoblanco
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villanueva De Córdoba traverse the diverse landscapes of the Los Pedroches Valley, characterized by extensive holm oak pastures (dehesa) and mountainous terrain. The region features natural environments like the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park and the Sierra de los Puntales, offering varied cycling experiences. Road cyclists can expect a mix of challenging climbs and scenic rides through Mediterranean forests and near the Guadalmellato Reservoir. Best road cycling routes around Villanueva De Córdoba The most popular road cycling route is Puerto El Caballón descends Villanueva de Córdoba — circular by Pozoblanco, a difficult 59.1 miles (95.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park Loop, a difficult 79.5 miles (127.9 km) path. This route offers challenging riding through the diverse scenery of the natural park. Local road cyclists also love the Villanueva de Córdoba, Conquista, and Cardeña Loop, a 46.1 miles (74.2 km) trail leading through the picturesque dehesa landscape, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Road cycling around Villanueva De Córdoba is defined by holm oak pastures, mountainous terrain, and natural parks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult ascents. The routes in Villanueva De Córdoba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Córdoba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Villanueva de Córdoba?
A: Road cycling routes in Villanueva de Córdoba traverse diverse landscapes, primarily characterized by extensive &lt;strong&gt;holm oak pastures (dehesa)&lt;/strong&gt; and mountainous terrain. You'll find a mix of challenging climbs and scenic rides through Mediterranean forests, offering varied experiences for road cyclists.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Villanueva de Córdoba?
A: While Villanueva de Córdoba offers many challenging routes, there are also moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008834189/torrecampo-from-villanueva-de-cordoba-circular-via-conquista" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrecampo from Villanueva de Córdoba — circular via Conquista&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 51.3 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1335260882/villanueva-de-cordoba-conquista-and-cardeña-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Córdoba, Conquista, and Cardeña Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 74.2 km trail, both suitable for those looking for less strenuous rides.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced road cyclists, Villanueva de Córdoba offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005465142/puerto-el-caballon-descends-villanueva-de-cordoba-circular-by-pozoblanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto El Caballón descends Villanueva de Córdoba — circular by Pozoblanco&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 95.1 km route with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016818272/sierra-de-cardena-y-montoro-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 127.9 km through diverse natural park scenery.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Villanueva de Córdoba?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005465142/puerto-el-caballon-descends-villanueva-de-cordoba-circular-by-pozoblanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto El Caballón descends Villanueva de Córdoba — circular by Pozoblanco&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1335260882/villanueva-de-cordoba-conquista-and-cardeña-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Córdoba, Conquista, and Cardeña Loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic loops through the landscape.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Villanueva de Córdoba?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through the extensive &lt;strong&gt;holm oak pastures (dehesa)&lt;/strong&gt;, explore the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, or ride near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/929932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalmellato Reservoir and Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also part of a &lt;strong&gt;Starlight Reserve&lt;/strong&gt;, offering exceptional stargazing opportunities if you're out after dark.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: While primarily known for its natural landscapes, Villanueva de Córdoba also has cultural sites. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4288368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna&lt;/a&gt;, a significant religious and cultural site, or pass through the town center where you can see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Villanueva de Córdoba?
A: The road cycling routes in Villanueva de Córdoba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Cyclists often praise the picturesque dehesa landscapes, the challenging climbs, and the diverse scenery found within the natural parks.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villanueva de Córdoba?
A: The region experiences hot summers, so spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for road cycling, offering milder temperatures. Winter can also be suitable, though some days might be cooler. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling options or greenways nearby?
A: Beyond the immediate routes, the wider Córdoba province offers longer cycling experiences. The &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite (Olive Oil Greenway)&lt;/strong&gt;, an old railway line, provides a 128 km route between Córdoba and Jaén, passing through olive groves. Another option is the &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde de la Campiña&lt;/strong&gt;, covering 55 km with minimal elevation change.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Villanueva de Córdoba?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around Villanueva de Córdoba, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to suit different preferences.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Sierra de los Puntales?
A: Yes, some routes will take you through the natural environment of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de los Puntales&lt;/strong&gt;, offering cycling through Mediterranean forest, often with minimal motorized traffic. This area also provides viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/874658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de los Puntales viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: A small square where the three symbols of the town are located (which also appear on the shield) - an oak, a well and a rooster. It is said that the first house in Pozoblanco was built here - the Casa de la Viga.

This rise starts at the passage of the Cuzna River, at the beginning it has strong slopes at 10/12% to continue with average slopes of 6%.
Beautiful port where the views of the mountains are spectacular.

In the central square of the town of Villanueva de Córdoba are the anti-aircraft shelter from the Civil War (it is the best preserved in the province) and the Church of San Miguel Arcángel (from the 16th century).

waypoints.name: Plaza de España in Villanueva de Córdoba
